goal: Build a simple, polished hackathon MVP that helps people find emergency resources during outages, heat waves, wildfires, or local emergencies.
core_pitch: ReliefRoute is a real-time emergency resource heatmap that connects people to nearby shelters, water stations, charging spots, Wi-Fi locations, clinics, and cooling centers. It recommends the best nearby resource using availability, distance, trust score, and service match.

tech_stack.frontend: Next.js with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
tech_stack.backend: Next.js API routes or server actions
tech_stack.maps: Google Maps API preferred, fallback UI allowed
tech_stack.auth: No full auth for MVP. Use anonymous localStorage userId for reports.

mvp_features: Seeded demo scenario for a Los Angeles power outage

flow: App recommends best open trusted resource

agent_workflow_rule: For each major part, use senior-architect first to inspect and plan. Then use the correct junior agent to implement. Then use debugger if anything fails. Then use qa-reviewer. End with handoff-manager.
no_hallucination_rule: Before making claims or editing code, inspect the actual files. Do not assume file names, components, APIs, schemas, or installed packages. If unsure, search the repo.
debug_rule: When errors happen, try up to 3 focused debugging attempts. Inspect the error and relevant files before editing. Apply minimal fixes. If unresolved, document the blocker in HANDOFF.md.
handoff_rule: At the end of every session, update HANDOFF.md with completed work, changed files, commands run, what works, what does not work, blockers, exact next task, and recommended next agent.
first_task: Start Part 1 only. Inspect the current folder. If no app exists, create a Next.js TypeScript Tailwind app structure. Build the landing page and basic UI shell. Use static demo data first. Do not implement MongoDB yet. End by creating HANDOFF.md.
